result:
Decisions filled at `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/agent-output/spider-support-givensspec-templates-embedded.yaml`.

**Shape of the answer set:**

- **Core design line**: keep the existing regex system (D1a), add `${...}` as the inline syntax (D3a), keep bare-$ whole-value alongside it rather than fold a migration into this commission (D2a) — principle #23 says the commission's question is "add inline support," not "migrate whole-value syntax."
- **Fail-loud posture**: D4a permissive-capture-then-validate, D6c throw on missing, D16a unknown namespaces are errors, D25a keep $-prefix-no-match as an error. Principle #2 runs through all of these.
- **No speculative structure**: D5a single-level (no nested dot-paths), D10a no sentinel marker, D15a fail-on-first (no error collection), D26a no escape mechanism. Principle #18 — earn it from a second consumer.
- **Helper boundary**: D20a shared helper, D21b inline only (whole-value stays in callers), D22a resolver callback (helper doesn't own lookup semantics). Principles #8 and #16 keep the helper's knowledge minimal.
- **One low-confidence abstain**: D13 (scan-all vs prefilter) — micro-perf/code-shape choice no principle speaks to.

No `custom` answers were needed — the option frames were well-posed and the principles landed cleanly on existing options.
